content: Autonomous AI agents can use biologically-inspired working memory retention gating mechanisms tied to intrinsic curiosity signals to dynamically allocate limited working memory capacity: only information that triggers novelty-seeking ICM curiosity is gated in for long enough retention to support multi-step reasoning, while trivial predictable details are immediately gated out to avoid capacity overload. This creates a self-regulating active memory system that doesn’t require external labeling to prioritize what to retain.
